summary: Fractures of gold dental restorations are mainly caused by issues related to their mechanical properties such as alloy composition, which affects ductility; improper design leading to high stress concentrations at margins or undercuts; and insufficient retention resulting from weak bonds or marginal gaps. All these factors collectively influence the likelihood of fracturing during use over time.

All these factors collectively influence the likelihood of fracturing during use over time.. Answer choice: D
